metadata.title: Instrument Connection Classification Rules
metadata.description: Classifies every ISA instrument type by whether it requires electrical cable connections, I/O points, or appears on Cable Schedules. Used by Project Intelligence, Tag Validator, and RD2 agents to distinguish real documentation gaps from expected behavior.
metadata.source: ISA 5.1-2024, field engineering standards, industry practice

never_has_cable.description: Purely mechanical instruments. No signal, no wire, no I/O, no Cable Schedule entry. If these appear in a Cable Schedule or I/O List, that is a DATA ERROR.

LG.name: Level Gauge / Sight Glass
LG.reason: Mechanical — operator reads liquid level visually through glass tube or magnetic indicators. No electronics.

PG.name: Pressure Gauge
PG.reason: Bourdon tube — mechanical needle deflection. Direct reading, no signal output.

TG.name: Temperature Gauge
TG.reason: Bimetallic dial — mechanical. Direct reading at the pipe.

TW.name: Thermowell
TW.reason: Metal pocket welded into pipe — holds a temperature sensor. The thermowell itself has no signal. The sensor inside (TE/TT) has the cable.

FE.name: Flow Element (Orifice Plate, Venturi, Flow Nozzle)
FE.reason: Passive restriction in pipe — creates differential pressure. No electronics. The DP transmitter (FIT/FT) connected to it has the cable.

RO.name: Restriction Orifice
RO.reason: Fixed flow restriction — purely mechanical plate in pipe. No signal.

PSV.name: Pressure Safety Valve / Relief Valve
PSV.reason: Spring-loaded mechanical — opens automatically on overpressure. No signal, no wire. Some facilities add a ZSO position switch to detect when PSV lifts, but the PSV itself has no cable.

PRV.name: Pressure Relief Valve
PRV.reason: Same as PSV — spring-loaded mechanical relief. No signal.

BD.name: Rupture Disc / Bursting Disc
BD.reason: One-time burst pressure relief — metal disc that ruptures. Destroyed on activation. No signal.

SS.name: Steam Strainer / Y-Strainer
SS.reason: Mechanical filter — catches debris. No signal.

HV.name: Hand Valve (manual, no actuator)
HV.reason: Manual handwheel operation — operator turns it physically. No cable unless it has an actuator (then it becomes XV/MOV).

always_has_cable.description: Electronic instruments that MUST have cable connections and I/O points. If these are MISSING from a Cable Schedule or I/O List, that is a real documentation gap.

transmitters.description: All transmitters send a continuous analog signal to DCS/PLC. They MUST appear in Cable Schedule (cable routing) and I/O List (DCS channel assignment).

controllers.description: Controllers are DCS functions — they receive transmitter input and send output to valves. The controller tag itself is a DCS point, so the associated transmitter and valve need cables.

control_valves.description: Control valves with positioners receive a 4-20mA signal from DCS to modulate position. They MUST appear in Cable Schedule and I/O List.

on_off_valves.description: On/off valves are actuated by solenoids (XY). The solenoid receives a discrete signal from DCS. Cable runs from JB to solenoid.

solenoids.description: Solenoid valve — converts electrical signal to pneumatic action. Actuates XV/SDV. Always has a cable.

switches.description: Process switches send ON/OFF signals to DCS when a process variable crosses a setpoint. Always wired.

hand_switches.description: Hand switches (start/stop buttons) — operator-actuated, wired to DCS/PLC.

position_transmitters.description: Position transmitter on valve actuator. Sends continuous valve position feedback to DCS. Always has a cable.

motor_instruments.description: Motor current indicators (II), speed indicators (SI), motor starters (YS) — all wired.

alarms.signal_type: DCS software function (derived from transmitter signal)
alarms.description: Alarms are typically DCS SOFTWARE points — they use the transmitter's existing signal, not a separate cable. PAH is triggered when PT exceeds setpoint. The alarm tag itself has NO separate cable. Exception: if the tag is actually a hardware alarm switch (rare), it has its own cable — but then it's really a PSH/TSH/LSH, not PAH.
alarms.note: Software alarm = no cable. Hardware alarm switch = use PSH/TSH/LSH tag instead.

depends_on_context.description: Instruments that may or may not have cables depending on whether they are local (field-mounted mechanical) or remote (wired to DCS). The P&ID symbol tells you: if drawn with a signal line to a DCS/PLC bubble, it has a cable. If standalone circle with no line, it's local/mechanical.

PI.name: Pressure Indicator
PI.local: Field-mounted pressure gauge — same as PG. No cable.
PI.remote: If wired to DCS (shown as PIT), has 4-20mA cable.
PI.rule: If PI appears alone on P&ID with no signal line → no cable needed. If it has a signal line to DCS → it's really a PIT and needs cable.

TI.name: Temperature Indicator
TI.local: Field-mounted dial thermometer — same as TG. No cable.
TI.remote: If wired to DCS (shown as TIT), has cable.
TI.rule: Same logic as PI. Local TI = no cable. Remote TI = cable.

LI.name: Level Indicator
LI.local: Magnetic level indicator or sight glass type — no cable.
LI.rule: Same logic. Most LI in the field are local. Check P&ID symbol.

FI.name: Flow Indicator
FI.local: Local rotameter or flow sight glass — no cable.
FI.remote: If remote reading via DCS → cable.
FI.rule: Local rotameters are mechanical. Check P&ID.

ZI.name: Position Indicator
ZI.local: Mechanical dial indicator on valve actuator — no cable. Operator reads position visually.
ZI.remote: If wired to DCS for remote position display → has cable (rare, usually use ZT instead).
ZI.rule: Most ZI in the field are local mechanical dials on actuators. No cable unless wired to DCS.

TE.name: Temperature Element (RTD/Thermocouple)
TE.local: The bare sensing element. Often shares the cable with its TT (transmitter). The TE itself does not have a SEPARATE cable schedule entry — the TT does.
TE.remote: If wired directly to DCS without a TT (head-mount transmitter), the TE has its own cable.
TE.rule: TE usually shares wiring with TT. Don't flag TE as missing cable if TT in the same loop has a cable.

PE.name: Pressure Element (sensing element)
PE.local: Primary sensing element — usually integral to PT. No separate cable.
PE.remote: Rare as a standalone. Usually part of PT assembly.
PE.rule: Same as TE — don't flag separately if PT has a cable.

AE.name: Analyzer Element (sensor/probe)
AE.local: In-situ probe (e.g., zirconia O2). Often wired as part of AT assembly.
AE.remote: If separate from AT, may have its own cable for heater power or signal.
AE.rule: Context-dependent. Usually the AT cable covers the AE.

one_line_elements.description: Elements that appear on ONE_LINE (single-line electrical) diagrams. These are POWER distribution, not instrumentation. They do NOT appear in instrument Cable Schedules — they appear in POWER cable schedules (electrical trade). Different document, different trade.

elements.M: Motor — power cable only, no instrument I/O (but monitoring instruments like VT, II, TSH are separate tags)
elements.VFD: Variable Frequency Drive — power + speed control signal (SIC). The VFD is power; the SIC is instrumentation.
elements.MCC: Motor Control Center — power distribution panel. No instrument I/O.
elements.CB: Circuit Breaker — protection device. Power only (some have status contacts for DCS monitoring).
elements.UPS: Uninterruptible Power Supply — power backup. No instrument I/O.
elements.GEN: Generator — power source. No instrument I/O (but has monitoring instruments).
elements.SWG: Switchgear — power switching. No instrument I/O.

one_line_elements.rule: ONE_LINE elements should NEVER appear in an instrument Cable Schedule. If they do, the data is mixing power and instrumentation — flag as error. Monitoring instruments (VT, II, TSH on motors) are separate tags that DO appear in instrument Cable Schedule.

cable_schedule_errors.description: A 'never_has_cable' instrument (LG, PG, TG, TW, FE, PSV, PRV, BD, RO, HV, SS) appears in the Cable Schedule
cable_schedule_errors.message_template: {tag} is a {instrument_name} — a mechanical instrument with no electrical connection. It should not appear in the Cable Schedule.

cable_schedule_errors.description: A ONE_LINE element (M, VFD, MCC, XFMR, CB) appears in the instrument Cable Schedule
cable_schedule_errors.message_template: {tag} is a power distribution element. It belongs in the electrical power cable schedule, not the instrument cable schedule.

missing_cable_rules.description: A transmitter (FIT, PT, TT, LT, AT, etc.) has no Cable Schedule entry
missing_cable_rules.message_template: {tag} is a {instrument_name} that sends a 4-20mA signal to DCS. It MUST have a cable schedule entry.

missing_cable_rules.description: A control valve (FV, TV, PV, LV) has no Cable Schedule entry
missing_cable_rules.message_template: {tag} is a control valve with a positioner. It receives a 4-20mA signal from DCS and MUST have cable documentation.

missing_cable_rules.description: A process switch (PSH, TSH, LSH, ZSC, ZSO, etc.) has no Cable Schedule entry
missing_cable_rules.message_template: {tag} is a process switch that sends a discrete signal to DCS. It should have a cable schedule entry.

missing_cable_rules.description: A local indicator (PI, TI, LI, FI) or local gauge (LG, PG, TG) has no Cable Schedule entry
missing_cable_rules.message_template: {tag} is a local indicator/gauge — no cable required. This is normal.
